design_philosophy: A premium, high-end fintech mobile app simulator built as a single HTML file. It must feel like Robinhood or Cash App—deep void backgrounds, glassmorphism cards, and striking electric neon accents. The app creates a sense of urgency around 'leaks' (wasted subscriptions) and satisfaction in 'sniping' (canceling) them.

tech_stack_instructions.styling: Tailwind CSS via CDN (<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>). Add custom configuration in the script tag.
tech_stack_instructions.icons: Use Phosphor Icons via CDN (<script src="https://unpkg.com/@phosphor-icons/web"></script>) or Lucide via CDN.
tech_stack_instructions.fonts: Google Fonts (Outfit for headings, Manrope for body)
tech_stack_instructions.file_structure: Everything MUST be inside a single file named `index.html`. Do not use React or external bundlers.

layout_specs.container: Flex container to center the app on desktop. App itself must be a mobile frame: max-w-[430px] w-full h-[932px] max-h-[100vh] relative overflow-hidden rounded-[3rem] border-[8px] border-zinc-900 shadow-[0_0_100px_rgba(57,255,20,0.1)] bg-[#0A0A0A].
layout_specs.padding: General screen padding: p-6. Bottom padding: pb-24 (to account for bottom nav).

SplashScreen.description: Full screen overlay with a glowing neon green 'S' or crosshair logo built with SVG.
SplashScreen.animation: Pulse animation on the logo, then fade-out and slide-up of the whole screen after 2.5 seconds.

slides: 1. Welcome to SubScrub - The Subscription Sniper
2. Identify the Leaks - We find subscriptions draining your wallet
3. One-Tap Cancel - Generate legally binding cancellation letters
4. Take Control - Let's scan your accounts
Onboarding.ui: Large kinetic typography (Outfit 800), neon green progress dots, massive 'START SCAN' primary button on the last slide.

ScanProgress.description: A dramatic scanning screen shown after onboarding.
ScanProgress.ui: A sweeping radar or vertical scanning line animation over a dark grid. Random company names flash on screen quickly. Takes 3 seconds, then transitions to Dashboard.

Dashboard.hero: Massive typography showing 'Total Monthly Waste: $124.97'. The number should have a text-shadow/glow effect in red (#FF3366) transitioning to green.
Dashboard.cards: List of 'Leaking' and 'Active' subscriptions. Use glassmorphism cards.
Dashboard.leak_card: Subscriptions marked as leaks MUST have an animated gradient border (red to orange) and a glowing red warning icon.

SubscriptionDetail.description: Slide-in panel when a subscription is clicked.
SubscriptionDetail.ui: Shows large cost breakdown, 'Months Subscribed', and a massive neon green 'SNIPE SUBSCRIPTION' button.
SubscriptionDetail.cancel_letter: Clicking 'Snipe' generates a formal cancellation letter template inside a text area based on the chosen jurisdiction (Settings).

Settings.ui: Jurisdiction picker for the cancellation letters: KVKK (Turkey), GDPR (Europe), Generic (US/Global). Simple toggle/radio UI.

BottomNav.ui: Fixed at the bottom of the mobile frame. Glassmorphism background. 3 icons: Dashboard (House), All Subs (List), Settings (Gear). Highlight active tab in neon green.

interactions_and_animations.buttons: All buttons must have a hover/active state (scale down to 0.95) and a glowing box-shadow.
interactions_and_animations.screen_transitions: Use simple CSS classes (.screen-enter, .screen-exit) for smooth crossfades and subtle Y-axis translations between Dashboard, Detail, and Settings.
interactions_and_animations.leak_border: Use a CSS conic-gradient animation via @keyframes for the leaking subscription cards to simulate a moving neon border.

testing.requirements: All interactive elements (buttons, nav items, cards) MUST include a `data-testid` attribute (e.g., `data-testid="nav-settings"`, `data-testid="sub-card-netflix"`).

instructions_to_main_agent: 2. Inject Tailwind via CDN `<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>` and configure the custom colors in `tailwind.config` inside the script.
3. Use standard CSS within `<style>` for custom animations (radar scan, glowing borders, screen transitions).
4. Use Vanilla JavaScript within `<script>` to handle DOM manipulation, screen switching, and the cancellation letter logic.
5. Hardcode all the demo data exactly as specified.
6. Build a beautiful, centered mobile frame on desktop, resembling a premium iPhone app.
7. Do NOT use React or build tools. Strictly single HTML.
